understanding.goal_understanding: The goal was to navigate two selectable pieces (one at [0, 24] and one at [18, 21]) to reach goal positions marked on the grid. The level required coordinating movement of both pieces, likely pushing blocks or maneuvering around walls to reach target cells indicated by '+' symbols in the semantic grid.
understanding.mechanics_understanding: Movement works on a 3-pixel grid system where pieces occupy pixel coordinates. Selecting pieces with CLICK switches control between selectables. Moving in cardinal directions (MOVE_LEFT/RIGHT/UP/DOWN) shifts the selected piece by 3 pixels in that direction. Pushable blocks (B objects) can be moved if a piece moves directly into them. Walls (#) block movement and create barriers. The movement_blocked hints indicate adjacent obstacles but don't guarantee failure. The puzzle required careful sequencing: moving one piece to create a path or push blocks into position, then switching to the other piece to progress. With only 1 step remaining after 49 turns and no goal achievement reported, the level was not completed before the budget exhausted.
